title: Platinum Artifact Collection And Analysis Dashboard
strapline: A dashboard-first intake and decision cockpit for current games and future additions: gather source artifacts, analyze them, turn them into a Platinum-focused instantiation plan, and keep conformance work grounded in durable evidence.
currentGoal: Make artifact collection and analysis the front door for every game on Platinum, with Aurora and Galaxy Guardians visible today and Windigo Invaders entering through a measured, source-first intake path.
dashboardRead: This page should be the primary place where new game materials are added, assessed, and turned into explicit build decisions. Read each game top-down as: current posture, current content package, how we will use that content now, and then the remaining missing artifacts that still matter enough to change gameplay quality or implementation confidence. It should stay compact at the top level, let us click into detail by game, and keep showing what source families are still missing before design outruns evidence.
lastUpdatedNote: Dashboard and repo policy now reflect a closed public/private build boundary and a new cross-machine cleanup checklist: the shipped reference-audio lane is private, `dist/` no longer republishes private-derived catalog media, and the next deliberate work is to integrate the other machine and then review mixed conformance lanes file by file.

phaseDefinitions.label: Artifact Collection
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Accession real source materials first: gameplay video, manuals, score tables, stills, cabinet art, and written reference pages.

phaseDefinitions.label: Analysis
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Turn sources into reviewable windows and evidence: contact sheets, waveforms, semantic notes, timing slices, and confidence gaps.

phaseDefinitions.label: Platinum Instantiation Plan
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Convert evidence into an explicit game plan that separates Platinum-owned host work from game-owned rules, timing, audio, and identity.

phaseDefinitions.label: Implementation
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Build only the smallest evidence-backed slice needed to prove the game on Platinum.

phaseDefinitions.label: Conformance
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Measure the game against its source lineage with runtime artifacts, personas, and visible review surfaces.

phaseDefinitions.label: Release Review
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Decide whether the game is ready for public claims, beta review, or continued private iteration.

artifactFamilies.label: Gameplay video
artifactFamilies.detail: Longplay, attract, operator, and targeted scenario windows with clear provenance and usable audio.
artifactFamilies.why: This is the main source for enemy movement, player pacing, score behavior, wave progression, and audio-event timing.
examples: One full or near-full longplay with original audio
One attract-mode or title/start sequence clip
One focused clip showing a single stage or scenario clearly
artifactFamilies.minimum: Enough to see the first 2-3 minutes clearly, hear real game audio, and study at least one representative stress window.

artifactFamilies.label: Manuals and rule documents
artifactFamilies.detail: Operator sheets, score tables, cabinet docs, and written descriptions of mechanics and progression.
artifactFamilies.why: These stop us from guessing scoring, bonus rules, player counts, lives, progression, and cabinet-facing messaging.
examples: Written gameplay overview from a reliable arcade reference
artifactFamilies.minimum: At least one trustworthy source for score values and one source for rules or operator-facing setup.

artifactFamilies.label: Still-image references
artifactFamilies.detail: Cabinet, bezel, title, score-table, sprite-family, cannon/ship, bunker, and HUD references.
artifactFamilies.why: Still images let us study visual identity without motion blur and are usually the fastest way to verify UI, sprite families, colors, and cabinet framing.
artifactFamilies.minimum: At least one clear title/HUD image plus one image set covering the main player and enemy families.

artifactFamilies.label: Audio references
artifactFamilies.detail: Isolated or cleaner windows for cue identity, tempo, density, and game-event timing.
artifactFamilies.why: Gameplay audio quality depends on hearing what happens between events, not just proving that a cue exists.
examples: Pressure or march loop windows with minimal commentary
artifactFamilies.minimum: At least one usable start/end phrase and one representative live-play scene with real pressure and fire density.

artifactFamilies.label: Semantic timing windows
artifactFamilies.detail: Event-labeled clips for start, fire, hit, descent, failure, transition, and results moments.
artifactFamilies.why: These become the contract slices we tune against when timing, pacing, or transitions drift.
examples: First enemy descent or pressure moment
artifactFamilies.minimum: At least 4-6 labeled clips covering start, pressure, hit, loss, and end-state behavior.

artifactFamilies.label: Runtime correspondence captures
artifactFamilies.detail: Persona-driven or manual recordings from Platinum that can be reviewed against the reference set.
artifactFamilies.why: Without Platinum-side comparison captures, we can’t tell whether the implementation is getting closer or merely changing.
artifactFamilies.minimum: At least one manual and one harness-driven capture once runtime work begins.

minimumIntakeChecklist.title: One authoritative longplay video
minimumIntakeChecklist.why: Needed to anchor pacing, wave structure, scoring rhythm, and live soundscape.
minimumIntakeChecklist.goodEnough: A preserved source package with checksum, manifest, contact sheet, and waveform.

minimumIntakeChecklist.title: One rules or operator source
minimumIntakeChecklist.why: Needed to keep lives, scoring, player count, and progression from becoming guesswork.
minimumIntakeChecklist.goodEnough: A manual page, operator sheet, or high-confidence rules page saved into the repo.

minimumIntakeChecklist.title: One score-table source
minimumIntakeChecklist.why: Needed to define exact hit values, bonus structure, and any special scoring behavior.
minimumIntakeChecklist.goodEnough: A still or document source with readable values and enough provenance to trust it.

minimumIntakeChecklist.title: One visual identity set
minimumIntakeChecklist.why: Needed to establish title, HUD, sprite families, player ship/cannon, and cabinet-facing presentation.
minimumIntakeChecklist.goodEnough: A small bundle of readable stills covering title, HUD, main enemies, and player object.

minimumIntakeChecklist.title: One event-labeled timing bundle
minimumIntakeChecklist.why: Needed so the first runtime slice can target real start, pressure, hit, loss, and end-state windows.
minimumIntakeChecklist.goodEnough: A short set of named clips or notes pointing to exact timestamps in preserved video.

globalHunts.title: Standing hunt
globalHunts.detail: Find manual and score-table sources early so scoring, bonus, and staging do not remain implied.

globalHunts.title: Standing hunt
globalHunts.detail: Prefer at least one longplay plus one cleaner short-window video per game line.

globalHunts.title: Standing hunt
globalHunts.detail: Promote audio windows that match real gameplay scenes, not only isolated cue existence.

globalHunts.title: Standing hunt
globalHunts.detail: Keep every game's plan explicit about what Platinum owns versus what the game owns.

globalHunts.title: Standing hunt
globalHunts.detail: If a missing artifact family repeatedly blocks conformance, add it to the standing intake requirement set.

platformExtensions.title: Generic preserved-source package contract
platformExtensions.detail: Every new game should enter through the same manifest, checksum, preview-artifact, and README pattern used here for Windigo Invaders.

platformExtensions.title: Generic audio scene and live-mix review
platformExtensions.detail: The new audio scene/live-mix analyzers should remain game-configured so a third game can use them without bespoke platform rewrites.

platformExtensions.title: Persona capture and review rail
platformExtensions.detail: Future games should inherit the same persona-driven capture, review, and replay discipline once their rules are mature enough.

platformExtensions.title: Shared motion grammar and routeability gates
platformExtensions.detail: Movement candidates should be described with common primitives, then promoted only when visual fit, safety, shot windows, and player routeability all hold together.

games.title: Aurora Galactica
games.themeRead: The most mature current game. Intake and conformance should stay visible because future games should inherit the process, not just the runtime.
games.lastUpdatedNote: Aurora status now reflects a fully closed obvious reference-media boundary: the large audio-fitting families, challenge/reference packs, legacy reference-audio/video residue, and even the old shipped reference-audio cue lane are private, while the public build now stays on repo-owned mixes and metadata. The next review is the mixed conformance lanes where runtime-safe evidence and source-derived comparison sheets still live together.

phaseStatus.summary: Reference and extracted media families are established.
Challenge, sprite, motion, and artifact scoring are already active.
Measured quality gaps are explicit and documented.
Still improving challenge-stage and motion fidelity.
Public family live, `/dev` remains the review lane.

headlineScores.label: Challenge-stage conformance
headlineScores.detail: Still the clearest weak user-facing area.

headlineScores.label: Human-perfect routeability guard
headlineScores.detail: Challenge candidates now fail promotion if they reduce strong-player scoring windows.

games.decisionRead: Aurora is now in the phase where the highest-value work is evidence conversion, not broad source hunting. We already have strong challenge-stage coverage, named audio-cue packs, supporting strategy/context sources, annotated still bundles, cabinet/spec grounding, and mature runtime-side conformance artifacts. That means the next big quality gains should come from cutting better challenge-family subclips, motion windows, and explosion-lifecycle still bundles from the sources we already own, then using those extracted evidence units to drive the next challenge-stage and impact-feedback passes.

planTracks.title: Keep Aurora as the process template
planTracks.goal: Future games should inherit Aurora's measurement discipline without inheriting Aurora-specific truth.
deliverables: Maintain cross-game catalog rows
Keep hosted docs aligned with current conformance artifacts
Preserve audio and motion harnesses as generic platform contracts where possible
Block visually tempting challenge candidates that reduce human-perfect routeability

planTracks.title: Convert owned sources into runtime-ready evidence units
planTracks.goal: Move Aurora's remaining weak areas by extracting more precise evidence from the sources we already have.
deliverables: Named challenge-family subclips
Frame-accurate flap and novelty motion windows
Boss-hit and explosion lifecycle still bundles

nextArtifacts.title: Extract one complete labeled subclip for each challenge-stage family under active tuning
nextArtifacts.why: We already have videos of every Aurora challenging stage. The gap is not basic coverage. The gap is that many comparisons still depend on long source videos instead of short, named subclips that isolate the exact set-piece being tuned.
find: For each tuned challenge family, cut one 8-20 second subclip that starts before the first enemy becomes visible and ends after the result hold settles
Include the full open, the full traversal pattern, and the full result banner/score hold in a single named clip
Name each clip by challenge family and visible pattern type, not only by stage number
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: At least one preserved extracted clip for each challenge family currently being tuned, with explicit start/end timestamps and a note explaining what visible set-piece it is meant to prove.

nextArtifacts.title: Promote frame-accurate motion windows for flap cadence and challenge-stage novelty
nextArtifacts.why: Aurora still needs better motion truth for bee/butterfly feel and for why one challenge pattern reads as a distinct spectacle instead of a generic enemy sweep.
find: Extract 2-6 second windows where a single bee or butterfly family is readable for at least 20 consecutive frames
Extract 4-10 second challenge windows where the novelty move is visually obvious, such as a fan, ladder, sweep, or cross-over pattern
Prefer windows with stable framing, minimal overlay obstruction, and no scene cut in the middle of the motion
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: A small reusable set of motion windows where frame-to-frame flap timing, spacing, and direction changes can be reviewed without scrubbing through full longplay footage.

nextArtifacts.title: Promote boss-hit and explosion lifecycle still bundles
nextArtifacts.why: Impact and explosion feedback remains the weakest broad artifact row.
find: One labeled frame sequence for a boss taking its first damaging hit
One labeled frame sequence for a full boss-destruction event
One labeled frame sequence for a normal enemy explosion from onset through decay
If possible, include stills that show exactly when score text does and does not appear during those events
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: At least one readable frame bundle for first-hit boss damage, one for full boss destruction, and one for a normal enemy explosion, each ordered so the onset, peak, and decay are obvious.

previews.label: Rejected challenge candidate before/after
previews.detail: Stage 3 candidate review sheet for a target-control candidate blocked by human-perfect routeability regression.

games.title: Galaxy Guardians
games.lineage: Galaxian-style second-game ingestion proof on Platinum
games.themeRead: The current visible second-game proof. It is strong enough to deserve deeper artifact discipline, but still weak enough that the dashboard should keep missing evidence and risk areas visible.
games.lastUpdatedNote: Guardians status now sits inside a broader proven private-companion workflow with the public build boundary closed too: clear source-derived residue and the old shipped reference-audio lane are private, and the remaining public-media question is narrower and more deliberate - mixed conformance lanes like galaxy-guardians-identity now need file-by-file review while repo-owned runtime evidence can stay public.

phaseStatus.summary: Multiple Galaxian-family sources and extracted windows already exist, now including a no-voiceover early-session gameplay clip, a full start-to-end 15-wave session, a named FLAC cue pack, an indexed sound reel, a sustained ambience source, and supporting prose/manual/link-harvest packages.
Audio, movement, long-surface, and visual identity artifacts are now substantial.
Top-ten and audio sprint plans exist, but deeper play quality still needs work.
Playable preview on Platinum with game-owned identity.
Reference and playtest reads are honest and still below public-release confidence.
Useful as a review artifact, not yet a mature standalone public promise.

headlineScores.label: Public readiness
headlineScores.detail: Still too low for a strong public promise.

headlineScores.label: Movement routeability
headlineScores.detail: New Guardians routeability review; weakest route is stage-five stress.

headlineScores.label: Motion grammar candidates
headlineScores.detail: Shared-grammar movement candidate queue; top item is stage-five routeability relief.

headlineScores.label: Audio reference comparison
headlineScores.detail: Good coverage, still weak direct acoustic proxy fit.

headlineScores.label: Audio live mix
headlineScores.detail: Live mix is now strong; source-faithful match is the remaining gap.

games.decisionRead: Guardians now has enough source truth to support a serious fidelity push rather than a generic intake pass. We have multiple measured gameplay sources across intro, early-session, and deeper-play continuity; named isolated core cues; a sustained ambience bed; a real arcade operator manual; official corroborating prose; better player-language about fairness and late-wave pressure; strong runtime-side acceptance artifacts; and now a first promoted evidence packet for early-session, stage-five-plus, motion, popup, manual-page, and still-image anchors. That means the next gameplay gains should come from using and expanding those promoted windows plus comparable runtime captures to drive audio authenticity, deeper-play fairness, motion cadence, and source-backed score/presentation behavior.

planTracks.title: Close the highest-value remaining evidence gaps
planTracks.goal: Expand the first promoted Guardians packet until the remaining documentary, ambience, and stage-five-plus correspondence gaps are no longer blocking high-confidence tuning.
deliverables: Expanded operator-manual page bundle with an explicit score-table anchor
Expanded cabinet-facing still bundle for control-panel, bezel, and marquee truth
Additional stage-five-and-beyond runtime correspondence captures
Residual ambience and static-card cue-map cleanup

nextArtifacts.title: Expand the extracted operator-manual page set with an explicit readable score-table page plus any remaining setup/rules anchors
nextArtifacts.why: The first promoted packet now includes readable operator-manual pages for general instructions, bonus-life setup, and a front cabinet picture. The remaining high-value manual gap is the explicit score-table anchor plus any additional page that materially sharpens rules or setup claims.
find: One readable score-table page capture from the manual
One readable controls or gameplay-rules page capture
One readable setup, coin, DIP, or service page capture
Any page that materially helps cabinet-facing title/control-panel or operating-language review
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: A small promoted manual-page bundle with at least score, rules, and setup represented, each saved as its own readable artifact with a short note explaining what claim it anchors.

nextArtifacts.title: Expand the cabinet-facing, control-panel, bezel, marquee, and related presentation still bundle beyond the first flyer/manual anchors
nextArtifacts.why: The first promoted packet now includes a Midway flyer anchor, a Galaxip still, a flagship-family still, and a front cabinet picture. The remaining still-image gap is narrower and more important for Guardians presentation: control-surface, marquee, and bezel truth is still mostly lead-level rather than preserved repo-owned evidence.
find: At least one clear cabinet-facing image from the linked cabinet or museum pages
At least one readable control-panel or instruction-surface image
At least one marquee or bezel image that helps cabinet-facing title and framing review
A small README or manifest that says which preserved image will anchor cabinet, control-panel, bezel, and marquee review
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: One small repo-owned still-image package containing at least one cabinet view, one control-panel or instruction surface, and one bezel or marquee anchor, each checksumed and named for later presentation and visual-conformance use.

nextArtifacts.title: Map only the remaining unidentified or non-FLAC regions in the static-card Galaxians sound reel
nextArtifacts.why: The new KHInsider FLAC pack already covers the strongest named isolated core cues. The older static-card reel now matters only where it still contains missing or ambiguous families that the FLAC pack does not cover.
find: Compare the reel against the eight named FLAC cues and mark which regions are now redundant
Identify any remaining regions that appear to represent non-FLAC families, extended variants, or a longer mixed gameplay segment
Assign timestamps and provisional names only to those residual regions
Preserve the result as a small timestamp ledger that explicitly says which gaps remain after the FLAC pack is considered
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: A short residual-gap cue map that explains what the static-card reel still contributes after the named FLAC pack is taken as the primary isolated core-cue source.

nextArtifacts.title: Promote a named recurring-pressure comparison window from the new ambience source
nextArtifacts.why: The generic source-side ambience-bed hunt is now largely closed, so the next high-value step is to turn this sustained source into a concrete comparison artifact for Guardians' ongoing background family.
find: Select one or two representative 10-20 second windows from the preserved ambience source
Use those windows as the reference anchor for Guardians' recurring pressure family and live listening review
Note whether any noticeable modulation or phrase change happens inside the bed so the runtime target is not flattened incorrectly
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: A small promoted ambience-window artifact set that can be used directly in the audio conformance sprint instead of treating the full 150-second source as one undifferentiated blob.

nextArtifacts.title: Expand stage-five-and-beyond runtime correspondence captures to at least one additional persona or manual review lane
nextArtifacts.why: The first current-pass runtime correspondence set now includes refreshed opening and stage-five professional captures. The weakest remaining gameplay gap is deeper-play survivability and fairness across more than one runtime lane.
find: At least one additional persona-driven capture that reaches stage 5 or later under the same general review conditions
At least one manual review capture where lower-field crowding, escape space, and recovery after a near miss are visible
Each capture should preserve score, stage reached, survival time, and whether the player died to crowding, shot density, or a readable mistake
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: At least one additional stage-5+ persona or manual review capture that lets us compare the current professional run against another deeper-play runtime lane instead of relying on a single midrun sample.

previews.label: Stage-five routeability before/after
previews.detail: Guardians planning chart: baseline routeability 4.5/10, best candidate 5.8/10, now informing the bounded preview relief.

games.title: Windigo Invaders
games.lineage: Space Invaders-derived future Platinum game, themed eventually around the Windigo legend
games.themeRead: This should enter through evidence first. The goal is not to rush a derivative shell, but to build the strongest artifact package and Platinum instantiation plan before design decisions harden.
games.lastUpdatedNote: Windigo now inherits a mature intake and build boundary: obvious reference-side contact sheets, waveforms, copied cue packs, and any future derived media belong in the private companion repo, while the public repo keeps manifests, plans, and public-safe status surfaces without reopening the policy question.

phaseStatus.summary: Gameplay, rules/service docs, a named classic sound pack, and a StrategyWiki flyer/sprite bundle are accessioned and preserved.
First contact sheet, waveform, written rules grounding, and isolated sound-vocabulary review are now in repo.
The initial Platinum-focused instantiation plan now has written score/rules anchors.
Do not build runtime slices before the initial evidence package is strong enough.
Scorers should come after the first explicit plan and runtime slice.
No public claim yet; this is still in intake.

headlineScores.label: Current phase
headlineScores.detail: The game is intentionally still pre-runtime.

headlineScores.label: Preserved source count
headlineScores.detail: Gameplay, rules/service, named sound, StrategyWiki visual/reference, supporting history-review, and museum/spec preserved-source packages are now in-repo.

headlineScores.label: First-pass previews
headlineScores.detail: Gameplay contact sheet, gameplay waveform, and sound-pack waveform review generated.

games.decisionRead: Windigo is still correctly in the pre-runtime phase. We now have enough evidence to harden the first real Platinum instantiation plan: a gameplay seed source, rules and service documents, a named sound pack, a StrategyWiki flyer/sprite bundle, strong supporting history/review prose, and a museum/spec package that locks the original control model, mono sound, and black-and-white-with-color-overlay display identity. The next best spend is still on finishing the evidence package, not coding. The missing artifacts now matter because they are the ones that will prevent false assumptions in score behavior, bunker wear, descent pressure, cabinet-facing presentation, and live gameplay audio timing.

planTracks.title: Build the initial Platinum instantiation plan
planTracks.goal: Separate what Platinum will host from what Windigo Invaders must own as game truth.
deliverables: Game-owned rules and score-table plan
Stage, bunker, cannon, and invader-family inventory
Audio, march, descent, and game-over timing targets

planTracks.title: Expand the artifact inventory before coding
planTracks.goal: Give the first runtime slice enough evidence that it feels deliberate rather than memory-driven.
deliverables: Manual and score-table artifacts
Cleaner audio windows
Higher-resolution gameplay windows
Still-image references for title, bunkers, cannon, and invader rows

planTracks.title: Finish the pre-runtime evidence package before implementation
planTracks.goal: Make the first Windigo runtime slice a measured translation of the source lineage instead of a prototype driven by remembered Space Invaders tropes.
deliverables: Original score-table truth
Higher-resolution gameplay-context audio windows
Bunker, cannon, and invader-row still bundles
Event-labeled timing clips for start, march, shot, hit, loss, and game over

nextArtifacts.title: Original score-table still or manual-page capture
nextArtifacts.why: We now have good written rules and scoring prose, but we still want stronger original-source score-table evidence before locking visible Windigo scoring claims.
find: Manual page with readable invader/UFO values
Attract or cabinet still showing score values
Photographic score-table reference
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: One readable original or near-original source that visually confirms the main score table.

nextArtifacts.title: Higher-resolution gameplay with cleaner audio
nextArtifacts.why: The current seed source is useful, and the new sound pack closes several isolated cue gaps, but we still need clearer gameplay-context footage to study bunker wear, descent pressure, and how those cues behave in live play.
find: Higher-resolution full gameplay video
Gameplay with clearer bunker erosion and invader-row reading
Live gameplay where the march, shot, hit, explosion, and UFO cues are heard in context
Attract/start sequence footage
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: One second longplay or focused video that is visibly clearer than the first seed and also useful for live audio-context review.

nextArtifacts.title: Preserve the marquee, cabinet, bezel, control-panel, and title/gameplay surfaces that remain after the new StrategyWiki bundle
nextArtifacts.why: The new StrategyWiki bundle now covers the flyer and core gameplay silhouettes. The remaining still-image gap is narrower and more important for Windigo presentation: cabinet-facing and title/gameplay surfaces are still mostly lead-level rather than preserved repo-owned evidence.
find: At least one clear marquee image
At least one cabinet-facing image and one bezel or control-panel image
One title/start or score-strip still and one representative gameplay screenshot
A small manifest that says which image anchors marquee, cabinet, bezel or control panel, and title/gameplay review
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: One small repo-owned still-image package containing marquee, cabinet, bezel or control panel, and title/gameplay images, each named and preserved for later Windigo presentation review.

nextArtifacts.title: Bunker still references
nextArtifacts.why: The new StrategyWiki bundle now closes the cannon and invader-family side of the silhouette problem. The remaining gameplay-silhouette gap is bunker-specific: we still need direct visual truth for bunker shape and erosion.
find: Close stills of bunkers
If possible, one or more stills showing partial bunker erosion
A small manifest that says which still anchors bunker shape and bunker-wear review
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: A small still bundle containing at least one intact bunker anchor and, ideally, one erosion example, each named and preserved for later Windigo gameplay-surface review.

nextArtifacts.title: Event-labeled timing clips for fire, hit, descent, and game over
nextArtifacts.why: These are now the slices we need to connect the preserved named sound pack to real gameplay timing and pressure.
find: Game start and first control handoff
Projectile fire and hit moments
March-cadence and alien descent or acceleration windows
Player loss and game-over windows
nextArtifacts.goodEnough: 4-6 named windows with timestamps or preserved extracted clips, including at least one start, one march sequence, one hit, and one loss or game-over scene.
